reasoning: The ICE BofA AA US Corporate Index yield dropped sharply at the end of February 2025, falling from ~4.97 on Feb 20 to 4.74 on Feb 28 alongside a broader bond rally and stock market selloff on slowing economic data. Because 2025-03-02 establishes a relatively low baseline, there is a strong probability of short-term mean reversion pushing the yield back above 4.74. The FRED trend analysis tool estimates a 66% chance of the yield exceeding 4.74 by 2025-03-09, which I use as my primary anchor for the first resolution date. Over the longer term, ongoing debates about Fed rate cuts and economic growth make the trajectory less certain; while rate cuts may push yields down, widening credit spreads from a slowing economy could offset this. In accordance with the instructions, uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, bringing the probabilities for more distant dates progressively closer to the base rate of 0.50.
